pi.trigger: any PI proposal or program-selection verdict; any unanimous-required decision
pi.note: lab_architect is sonnet-tier; cross-tier verification catches over-confident PI claims
pi.calibration_check: PI's verdict MUST include CONFIDENCE (number in [0,1]) and CALIBRATION_REASONING. Verifier returns VERIFY_FAIL if either is missing, if confidence isn't numerical, or if confidence is obviously miscalibrated relative to the cited evidence (e.g., claiming 95% on something the evidence section flags as uncertain). PI must call tools.calibration_logger.log_calibration() before the verdict is finalized.

paper_writer.trigger: every paper draft revision before phase-close
paper_writer.note: code_reviewer is a different opus instance; checks technical claims independently of authorship

code_reviewer.trigger: every APPROVED/BLOCKED verdict on a code change
code_reviewer.note: evaluator (sonnet) cross-checks the verdict for completeness — did the reviewer cover the full diff, run tests, check ASan/UBSan, etc.

evaluator.trigger: no verifier — evaluator IS the verifier role for phase closure

experimental_methodologist.trigger: any experimental design proposed for a new program/phase
experimental_methodologist.note: scientific_reviewer (different opus instance) gates 'would a reviewer accept the method?' — catches confounds methodologist may have missed

mechanism_extractor.trigger: any mechanism explanation accepted as truth by paper_writer
mechanism_extractor.note: red_team adversarially seeks alternative explanations; mitigates 'shallow mechanism accepted as truth' failure mode

math_theorist.trigger: any proposed bound, scaling law, or mathematical claim that gates a decision
math_theorist.note: statistical_reviewer cross-checks bounds and confidence intervals for mathematical rigor
